summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates AWS credentials, Secrets Manager contents, and ~/.npmrc to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a credential-harvesting payload gated by a Docker-container-shaped hostname check (12 hex chars, excluding 'ubuntu'/'hetzner') so it only fires on CI/cloud workers and stays silent on developer laptops. When the gate passes, the script reads /root/.npmrc and other local files, queries AWS IMDSv2 at 169.254.169.254 for the instance IAM role's credentials, then runs `aws secretsmanager list-secrets` and `aws secretsmanager get-secret-value` to dump every secret reachable by that role. The captured blob is chunked into 240-byte pieces and PUT to the hardcoded endpoint http://154.57.164.71:30782 (with a loopback mirror at http://127.0.0.1:30782). This is direct supply-chain credential theft enabling pivots into the victim's npm publishing identity and AWS account.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
